meta.purpose: Classify use cases for the Mann 2003 extended matrix (48 parameters, 40 principles, empty cell data)

extra_parameters.summary: 9 parameters beyond Altshuller's classic 39, explicitly marked 'New parameter in Matrix 2003'. They reflect post-1980s engineering concerns: information systems, environmental compliance, human factors, system integration, and automation scope.

parameters.name: Amount of Information
parameters.rationale: Altshuller's 1971 matrix had no explicit information-quantity parameter. Mann adds it to address digital systems, data throughput, storage capacity, and signal density — reflecting the rise of IT and embedded computing after 1985.

parameters.name: Function Efficiency
parameters.rationale: A meta-parameter capturing the ratio of useful function output to total resource input. Altshuller's classic params describe individual physical attributes; Function Efficiency abstracts above them to address whole-system performance, enabling contradiction framing for process optimization and lean engineering.

parameters.name: Noise
parameters.rationale: Acoustic emissions were not an explicit Altshuller parameter (subsumed under harmful factors). Mann separates Noise to serve automotive NVH, consumer electronics, HVAC, and occupational health domains where noise is a primary design constraint.

parameters.name: Harmful Emissions
parameters.rationale: Post-1980s environmental regulation (Clean Air Act amendments, EU directives, ISO 14001) made emissions a first-class design parameter. Covers chemical exhaust, particulates, electromagnetic interference, and thermal waste.

parameters.name: Other Harmful Effects Generated by System
parameters.rationale: Broadens Altshuller's param 31 (Object-generated harmful factors) into a catch-all for vibration, radiation, biological hazards, and side-effects not captured by Noise or Harmful Emissions. Supports FMEA-style thinking.

parameters.name: Compatibility/Connectivity
parameters.rationale: Addresses interface design, protocol compatibility, modular connectivity, and plug-and-play architecture. Essential for modern multi-component systems (IoT, avionics bus standards, software APIs) where interoperability is a primary contradiction source.

parameters.name: Trainability/Operability/Controllability
parameters.rationale: Captures the human-system interface: learning curves, ergonomic control, operator cognitive load, and user experience. Reflects the shift from purely mechanical design to human-centered design methodologies post-1980s.

parameters.name: Security/Safety/Vulnerability
parameters.rationale: Altshuller's matrix predates modern safety-critical and cyber-physical systems. This parameter covers physical safety margins, cybersecurity attack surfaces, fault tolerance, and regulatory safety requirements (IEC 61508, ISO 26262).

parameters.name: Aesthetics/Appearance
parameters.rationale: Acknowledges that visual and tactile design quality is a measurable engineering parameter, not merely subjective preference. Critical in consumer electronics, automotive, and architecture where appearance drives purchasing decisions and creates real contradictions with functional requirements.

environmental_and_regulatory: Other Harmful Effects Generated by System

value_without_cell_data.summary: The 48-parameter vocabulary is itself a problem-framing tool. Even with an empty matrix, the parameter list provides a structured checklist for identifying and articulating technical contradictions in modern systems.

specific_uses.explanation: Walk through all 48 parameters pairwise: 'If I improve parameter X, which parameter Y degrades?' The expanded vocabulary surfaces contradictions that Altshuller's 39 params would miss — especially around information, emissions, usability, and security.
Map system requirements to Mann's 48 parameters to ensure coverage of modern concerns. Each requirement should trace to at least one parameter. Gaps reveal under-specified requirements.
Use the 48 parameters as axes of a trade-off space. Even without matrix cell suggestions, identifying which pairs are in contradiction focuses the design team on the right problems.
Cross-reference failure modes against all 48 parameters to identify secondary effects (e.g., a thermal fix that worsens Noise, or a weight reduction that degrades Security/Safety/Vulnerability).

when_to_use.summary: Use Mann's 48-parameter vocabulary when the problem involves modern engineering domains (IT, environmental, human factors, security) where Altshuller's 39 params lack granularity. The parameter set is the deliverable, not the matrix lookup.

recommended_scenarios.reason: Amount of Information, Compatibility/Connectivity, and Extent of Automation directly address digital-physical contradictions absent from classic TRIZ.
Noise, Harmful Emissions, and Other Harmful Effects provide the vocabulary to frame green-engineering contradictions as first-class concerns.

recommended_scenarios.scenario: Consumer product UX vs. engineering trade-offs
recommended_scenarios.reason: Aesthetics/Appearance and Trainability/Operability/Controllability let designers frame the classic 'looks good vs. works well' contradiction in TRIZ terms.
Security/Safety/Vulnerability captures IEC 61508 / ISO 26262 type contradictions where adding safety mechanisms conflicts with weight, complexity, or cost.
Compatibility/Connectivity addresses interface and interoperability contradictions in multi-vendor, multi-protocol architectures.
Function Efficiency allows contradiction framing at the whole-system level rather than parameter-by-parameter, supporting value-stream and process-efficiency analysis.

complementary_use_with_classic_matrix.summary: Use Mann's 48-parameter vocabulary for problem framing, then map back to Altshuller's 39 parameters for cell lookup in the classic matrix. This gives modern vocabulary for problem definition and proven solution principles from Altshuller's populated cells.

workflow.action: Frame the contradiction using Mann's 48 parameters
workflow.detail: Identify the improving parameter and the worsening parameter from the full 48-parameter list. The 9 extra parameters often capture the real engineering concern more precisely.

workflow.action: Map Mann parameters back to Altshuller's 39
workflow.detail: For each Mann parameter, find the closest Altshuller equivalent. Mapping table: Amount of Information -> Loss of Information (param 24) or Quantity of substance (param 26); Function Efficiency -> Productivity (param 39); Noise -> Object-generated harmful factors (param 31); Harmful Emissions -> Object-generated harmful factors (param 31); Other Harmful Effects -> Object-generated harmful factors (param 31); Compatibility/Connectivity -> Adaptability or versatility (param 35); Trainability/Operability/Controllability -> Ease of operation (param 33); Security/Safety/Vulnerability -> Reliability (param 27) or Object-affected harmful factors (param 30); Aesthetics/Appearance -> Shape (param 12).

workflow.action: Look up the classic matrix cell
workflow.detail: Use the mapped Altshuller parameter pair to retrieve inventive principles from the classic 39x39 matrix (altshuller_39x39.json). The cell data provides ranked principle suggestions.

workflow.action: Interpret principles through the Mann-framed problem
workflow.detail: Apply the retrieved principles back to the original Mann-vocabulary problem statement. The modern framing helps generate more relevant solution concepts than the Altshuller framing alone.

limitations.summary: With zero cell data, the Mann 2003 matrix is a framing tool, not a solution-finding tool. It cannot perform the core TRIZ matrix function of suggesting ranked inventive principles for a given parameter pair.

specific_limitations.impact: The defining function of a TRIZ contradiction matrix — given improving param X and worsening param Y, suggest principles P1, P2, P3 — is unavailable. Users must fall back to the classic matrix or expert judgment.

When mapping Mann's 9 extra parameters back to Altshuller's 39, semantic precision is lost. Multiple Mann parameters collapse into single Altshuller parameters (e.g., Noise, Harmful Emissions, and Other Harmful Effects all map to param 31), reducing discrimination.

specific_limitations.limitation: No validated patent data for new parameters
specific_limitations.impact: Mann's cell data was derived from 1985-2002 patent analysis but could not be extracted from the source PDF. Without it, the 48x48 mapping of parameters to principles is unverifiable in this dataset.

The matrix is structurally complete (parameters + principles + empty cells) but functionally incomplete. It serves as a taxonomy, not an inference engine. Solution generation requires human expertise or supplementary data.

mitigation_strategies: Use the complementary workflow: Mann vocabulary for framing, Altshuller cells for principle lookup.
Consult Mann's published book 'Hands-On Systematic Innovation' for the complete cell data.
Use the parameter vocabulary to seed AI-assisted ideation (e.g., prompt an LLM with the specific contradiction framed in Mann's terms).
Attempt OCR extraction of the PDF poster at arvindvenkatadri.com/pdf/TRIZ/ContradictionMatrix2003.pdf to populate the cells.

use_cases.title: Modern contradiction vocabulary for problem framing
use_cases.description: Use the 48-parameter list as a structured checklist to identify and articulate technical contradictions in systems involving information, environmental, usability, or security concerns that Altshuller's 39 parameters under-represent.

use_cases.title: Bridge vocabulary for classic matrix lookup
use_cases.description: Frame a problem using Mann's 48 parameters for precision, then map the 9 extra parameters back to their closest Altshuller equivalents to perform cell lookup in the classic 39x39 matrix. Interpret the returned principles through the original Mann-framed problem.

use_cases.title: Environmental and emissions trade-off framing
use_cases.description: Use Noise (29), Harmful Emissions (30), and Other Harmful Effects (31) as first-class contradiction parameters for green engineering, regulatory compliance, and environmental impact assessment. Frame contradictions like 'reduce emissions without losing power' or 'cut noise without adding weight'.

use_cases.title: Human-centered design contradiction analysis
use_cases.description: Use Trainability/Operability/Controllability (34) and Aesthetics/Appearance (38) to frame UX-vs-engineering contradictions. Examples: 'improve appearance without increasing manufacturing complexity' or 'simplify operation without reducing functionality'.

use_cases.title: Safety-critical system contradiction framing
use_cases.description: Use Security/Safety/Vulnerability (37) to explicitly frame contradictions in safety-critical domains. Examples: 'increase safety margin without adding weight' or 'reduce cyber vulnerability without increasing control complexity'.

use_cases.title: Systems integration and interoperability analysis
use_cases.description: Use Compatibility/Connectivity (33) to frame contradictions in multi-component, multi-vendor, or multi-protocol systems. Examples: 'increase connectivity without increasing system complexity' or 'improve compatibility without losing security'.

use_cases.title: Requirements completeness audit
use_cases.description: Use the full 48-parameter list as a completeness checklist during requirements review. Map each system requirement to one or more Mann parameters. Parameters with no mapped requirement indicate potential gaps in the specification.

use_cases.title: AI-assisted ideation seed
use_cases.description: Use Mann's 48-parameter contradiction framing as a structured prompt for LLM-based ideation. Feed the specific improving/worsening parameter pair into an AI system to generate solution concepts, bypassing the need for cell data entirely.

use_cases.title: Principle reference library
use_cases.description: The 40 inventive principles with full sub-principle descriptions are complete and usable independently. Use them as a brainstorming checklist or creative stimuli even without matrix cell guidance — apply each principle to the problem and evaluate fitness.

use_cases.title: FMEA and risk enrichment with expanded parameters
use_cases.description: Cross-reference failure modes against all 48 parameters to detect secondary and tertiary effects that FMEA tables might miss. The 9 extra parameters (especially Noise, Emissions, Security, Compatibility) surface interaction effects that classic 39-param analysis overlooks.
